other_observers: Brian Gibbons John Maynard Dan Maynard; I saw it first, as I flushed it when my lead car drove past it, but we all saw it almost immediately after it flew up from the ground.
specific_location: field due east of Chico Basin Ranch Headquarters
description: Easy to identify, this long-tailed bird (tail approaching nine inches) had a face including chin and throat, white as was breast, salmon pink belly. The tails are distinctive and in flight they spread and scissored while the bird foraged. Dark wings and bill contrast with of light gray body.   See photo.Behaviors: Foraged actively in a mostly bare ground winter feeding pasture for cattle. Perhaps because of the high density of cow and horse droppings, there appeared to be plenty of insects on the ground for the bird to catch.   Perched mostly on low bushes but also on lower limbs of trees.Call: none heardPlumage: breeding
